text:
Once upon a time, Fred woke up to find an envelope taped to his window. Intrigued, he opened it. Inside lay a strange purple feather.

That night, Fred dreamed he entered a forest resembling memories he hadn't saved. The trees whispered secrets with leaves like confetti. Fred discovered glowing orbs that sang songs of forgotten joys.

In the morning, Fred decided to wear the feather. To his delight, people stopped to admire its unusual charm.

Their admiration turned into an uproar—not for Fred—it became Fred-hero fever.

A charity race swept through town. People donated feathers.

When Fred won a grand prize at an expo, the feather became national news. He wore it proudly everywhere.

It wasn't long before Fred realized he missed the whispers.

You see, the forest didn’t miss him. They watched him stroll through life, feathers fluttering gently.

Each evening, they sang his favorite tunes.

And Fred slept peacefully.

That’s just the feather tale. Fred learned that whispers
